_comment: GENERIC default record-field paths. Because Algolia is schemaless, only the most universal e-commerce fields (price, compare-at price, availability) get a best-guess default path. Everything else defaults to "NONE" (treated as a neutral/absent signal so scoring degrades gracefully instead of guessing a wrong index-specific path). To map the NONE fields for a specific index, run with --field-map <file> --sample-record <records.json> so the discovery pass can suggest the right paths, then set source to "confirmed" to lock them.

compareAtPrice.description: Original / compare-at price used to compute discount depth (numeric, 0 = no discount)

inventoryAvailable.description: Boolean / flag: true if the product is available for purchase

inventoryMap.description: Object of { storeId: quantity } for per-store stock counts — index-specific, map via discovery

recentlyOrderedCount.description: Number of recent orders — proxy for demand. Index-specific, map via discovery

reviewAverage.description: Average star rating (0-5). Index-specific, map via discovery

reviewCount.description: Number of reviews. Index-specific, map via discovery

collections.description: Array of collection/category names for merch scoring. Index-specific, map via discovery

waysToShop.description: Array of merchandising tags (e.g. "best seller", "new arrival"). Index-specific, map via discovery

promoTags.description: Array/flag of promotional tags (more tags = higher merch score). Index-specific, map via discovery

swatchJson.description: JSON array of swatch/variant options — bonus when 3+ present. Index-specific, map via discovery

productType.description: Flat product-type string, one input for category detection. Index-specific, map via discovery

_categoryHayFieldsNote: categoryHayFields is a list of canonical field keys (or literal dot-paths) whose values are concatenated to form the haystack for category detection. Add/remove entries here to tune what drives category detection. Unlike other keys, this is not resolved via the canonical key -> path lookup - it is resolved directly against the record using resolveRecordField.
